title: Metal working machine operatives
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Construct, assemble, maintain, and repair stationary steam boilers and boiler house auxiliaries. Align structures or plate sections to assemble boiler frame tanks or vats, following blueprints. Work involves use of hand and power tools, plumb bobs, levels, wedges, dogs, or turnbuckles. Assist in testing assembled vessels. Direct cleaning of boilers and boiler furnaces. Inspect and repair boiler fittings, such as safety valves, regulators, automatic-control mechanisms, water columns, and auxiliary machines. Fabricate, assemble, install, and repair sheet metal products and equipment, such as ducts, control boxes, drainpipes, and furnace casings. Work may involve any of the following: setting up and operating fabricating machines to cut, bend, and straighten sheet metal; shaping metal over anvils, blocks, or forms using hammer; operating soldering and welding equipment to join sheet metal parts; or inspecting, assembling, and smoothing seams and joints of burred surfaces. Includes sheet metal duct installers who install prefabricated sheet metal ducts used for heating, air conditioning, or other purposes. (Based on 51 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Perform preventive maintenance and repair on machines and equipment, such as cleaning, lubricating, and adjusting parts, as well as disassembling malfunctioning equipment to remove, repair, or replace defective parts using hand and power tools. Keep records of maintenance and inspections. Inspect, test, and evaluate equipment performance, and document results.

Read and interpret engineering drawings, diagrams, and specifications to determine needed repairs, fabrication methods, and operational sequences. Study blueprints or layouts of metal workpieces to determine grinding procedures, plan machine setups, and produce specified cuts.

Set up and operate machining and grinding machines to shape and finish metal workpieces, ensuring surfaces and dimensions meet specifications. This involves selecting and mounting appropriate tools, filing or finishing surfaces, and monitoring machine operations for necessary adjustments.

Inspect and repair metal fittings and components, including welding or other mechanical repairs to address defects. This includes examining workpieces for quality and compliance with specifications, reworking parts as necessary, and ensuring proper operation of machinery and equipment.

Fabricate and assemble metal products using techniques such as cutting, bending, and soldering. This may involve working with sheet metal and installing components like ducts, control boxes, or boiler fittings.
Document inspection results and work performed, and maintain accurate records of repairs and parts requisitioned. Provide estimates for repair costs and time required for completion of tasks.
Supervise or advise subordinate personnel during metal fabrication and repair operations to ensure adherence to safety regulations and operational efficiency.
Use precision measuring instruments to ensure that dimensions and specifications are met during machining or assembly processes, which ensures quality control of metal workpieces.

Test assembled equipment or metal components to ensure optimal performance and compliance with required safety and operational standards. This may include pressure testing for safety valves and regulators.
Assist in the installation of fabricated metal products and components based on specification and operational requirements. This often involves physical assembly or integration into existing systems.
Conduct quality assurance checks to evaluate the serviceability and performance of metal products and equipment, ensuring compliance with industry standards.

Select appropriate tools, safety equipment, and parts according to job requirements, and use hand and power tools such as screwdrivers, pliers, wrenches, and welding equipment for metal working tasks.
Perform routine maintenance on equipment and machinery, including changing oil, lubricating parts, and inspecting for proper operating condition.
Inspect, repair, and maintain automotive and mechanical equipment and machinery, ensuring that all components are functioning correctly and meet specified dimensions.
Disassemble, overhaul, and rebuild mechanical units such as engines, pumps, and transmissions, replacing or repairing parts as needed.
Create and modify jewelry designs, including pouring molten metal into molds and cutting and filing jewelry pieces to shape them as specified.
Examine and grade gemstones, evaluate clarity and quality, and ensure proper setting and mounting of stones in jewelry designs.
Inspect, test, and adjust vehicle systems such as steering mechanisms and brake systems to ensure proper operation and safety.
Repair or replace damaged components in vehicles, including engines, electrical systems, and body panels, utilizing appropriate tools and techniques.
Clean and tidy work areas, ensuring that tools and equipment are maintained and organized for optimal workflow.
Confer with customers to discuss vehicle issues and communicate repair needs, ensuring customer satisfaction and understanding of services performed.
Create jewelry from materials such as gold, silver, platinum, and precious or semiprecious stones.
Smooth soldered joints and rough spots using hand files and emery paper, and polish smoothed areas with polishing wheels or buffing wire.
Dispose of hazardous waste in an appropriate manner.
Use a brush to hand-paint areas in need of retouching or unreachable with a spray gun.
Remove grease, dirt, paint, or rust from surfaces in preparation for paint application using abrasives, solvents, brushes, blowtorches, washing tanks, or sandblasters.
Sand and apply sealer to properly dried finish.
Set up portable equipment such as ventilators, exhaust units, ladders, or scaffolding.
Clean equipment and work areas.
Perform routine maintenance such as cleaning and oiling parts, honing cylinders, and tuning ignition systems.
Observe machine operations, gauges, or thermometers, and adjust controls to maintain specified conditions.
Examine completed objects to determine thicknesses of metal deposits, or measure thicknesses using instruments such as micrometers.
Remove excess materials or impurities from objects using air hoses or grinding machines.
Inspect assembled vessels or individual components, such as tubes, fittings, valves, controls, or auxiliary mechanisms, to locate any defects.
Bolt or arc weld pressure vessel structures and parts together using wrenches or welding equipment.
Shape or fabricate parts such as stacks, uptakes, or chutes to adapt pressure vessels, heat exchangers, or piping to premises, using heavy-metalworking machines.
Load and adjust workpieces onto equipment or work tables using hand tools.
Maintain production records.
Repair and maintain equipment, objects, or parts, using hand tools.
Select gauges or types of sheet metal or nonmetallic material according to product specifications.
Dismantle engines using hand tools and examine parts for defects.
Record repairs made, time spent, and parts used.
Transport prefabricated parts to construction sites for assembly and installation.
Perform equipment maintenance, such as cleaning tanks and lubricating moving parts of conveyors.
Measure or weigh materials using rulers, calculators, and scales.

Assemble pattern sections, using hand tools, bolts, screws, rivets, glue, or welding equipment. Lay out and draw or scribe patterns onto material, using compasses, protractors, rulers, scribes, or other instruments. Clean and finish patterns or templates, using emery cloths, files, scrapers, and power grinders. Construct platforms, fixtures, and jigs for holding and placing patterns. Mark identification numbers or symbols onto patterns or templates. Select pattern materials such as wood, resin, and fiberglass. Apply plastic-impregnated fabrics or coats of sealing wax or lacquer to patterns. Paint or lacquer patterns. Study blueprints, sketches, models, or specifications to plan sequences of operations for fabricating tools, dies, or assemblies. Verify dimensions, alignments, and clearances of finished parts for conformance to specifications using measuring instruments. Devise and construct tools, dies, molds, jigs, fixtures, or modify existing tools and equipment. Consult and confer with engineering personnel to discuss developmental problems and to recommend product modifications. Lie out and mark reference points and dimensions on materials. Conduct test runs with completed tools or dies to ensure that parts meet specifications, making adjustments as necessary.

Align, fit, and join parts using bolts and screws or by welding or gluing. Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, drill presses, punch presses, or bandsaws, to fabricate prototypes or models. Cut, shape, and form metal parts using lathes, power saws, snips, power brakes and shears, files, and mallets. Drill, countersink, and ream holes in parts and assemblies for bolts, screws, and other fasteners. Grind, file, and sand parts to finished dimensions. Set up and operate conventional or computer numerically controlled machine tools such as lathes, milling machines, or grinders. Smooth and polish flat and contoured surfaces of parts or tools, using scrapers, abrasive stones, files, emery cloths, or power grinders. Fabricate new metal parts using drill presses, engine lathes, and other machine tools. Repair, fabricate, and install fittings using blueprints, shop sketches, and instruction manuals. Fabricate needed parts or items from sheet metal. Inspects and test products to verify conformance to specifications using precision measuring instruments or circuit testers.

Inspect finished dies for smoothness, contour conformity, and defects. Analyze test results, machine error messages, or information obtained from operators to diagnose equipment problems. Diagnose faults or malfunctions to determine required repairs using engine diagnostic equipment. Record details of repairs made and parts used. Maintain, repair, and overhaul industrial production or processing machinery or equipment. Examine and listen to equipment, read inspection reports, and confer with customers to locate and diagnose malfunctions. Test mechanical products and equipment after repair or assembly to ensure proper performance and compliance with specifications. Repair or replace defective or worn parts using hand tools, milling and woodworking machines, lathes, welding equipment, grinders, or saws. Collaborate with other workers to repair or move machines, machine parts, or equipment.

Reassemble machines and equipment following repair; testing the operation and making adjustments as necessary. Repair, maintain, and clean equipment, machinery and components including lubricating, adjusting, and replacing parts. Record production, repair, and maintenance information; keep service records for machines. Clean and lubricate parts as part of routine maintenance work on equipment and vehicles. Inspect or test damaged machine parts, and mark defective areas or advise supervisors of repair needs. Operate and inspect machines or heavy equipment to diagnose defects. Adjust, maintain, and repair or replace subassemblies using hand tools, jacks, and cranes. Test and replace electrical components and wiring using test meters, soldering equipment, and hand tools.

Cut and weld metal to repair broken metal parts, fabricate new parts, or assemble new equipment. Direct workers who are assembling or disassembling equipment or cleaning parts. Transport machine parts, tools, equipment, and other materials between work areas. Inventory and requisition machine parts, equipment, and other supplies to maintain and replenish stock.

Start machines and observe mechanical operation to determine efficiency and to detect problems. Reassemble equipment after completion of inspections, testing, or repairs, and signal crane operator to lower basic assembly units. Clear and discard worn machine parts and other refuse to maintain machinery and work areas. Adjust controls of machines according to production specifications. Transport machinery and equipment using hoists, dollies, rollers, and trucks. Assemble machines and fasten them to foundation or other structures using hand tools and power tools. Lay out mounting holes, using measuring instruments, and drill holes with a power drill.

Remove hardened material from machines or machine parts using abrasives, power and hand tools, jackhammers, sledgehammers, or other equipment. Perform other maintenance work as required in support of industrial operations.
Install door hardware; locks and closers. Install alarm and electronic access systems.
Level bedplate and establish centerline, using straightedge, levels, and transit.
Dismantle machinery or equipment for shipment or repair, using hammers, wrenches, crowbars, and other hand tools.
Shrink-fit bushings, sleeves, rings, liners, gears, and wheels to specified items, using portable gas heating equipment.
Construct foundation for machines, using hand tools and building materials such as wood, cement, and steel.
Operate engine lathe and other machines to grind, file, and turn parts to meet dimensional specifications.
Conduct preventative maintenance and repair, lubricate machines and equipment, and perform minor machine maintenance, such as oiling.
Weld, repair, and fabricate equipment or machinery; fit and assemble parts to make or repair machine tools.
Select, install, and adjust alignment of drills, cutters, dies, guides, and holding devices using templates and measuring instruments.
Monitor machine operations and inspect workpieces for defects, ensuring conformance to specifications using precision instruments.
Read blueprints or job orders to determine product specifications, tooling instructions, and plan operational sequences.
Fit and align fabricated parts to be welded or assembled, and mark curves, lines, holes, dimensions, and welding symbols onto workpieces.
Remove burrs, sharp edges, rust, or scale from workpieces, using files, hand grinders, or power tools.
Operate machines to saw, cut, shear, slit, punch, crimp, notch, bend, or straighten metal or plastic material; start and monitor machine operations, and record operational data.
Set machine stops or guides and turn controls to engage automatic feeds and set cutting speeds, feed rates, or table angles.
Dismantle or remove defective parts or tools and replace them with new or repaired items, using hand tools.
Lift and position workpieces in relation to surface plates, manually or with hoists, using parallel blocks and angle plates.
Diecut, trim, compress, and verify workpieces to ensure conformance to product specifications, using measuring instruments.
Confer with other workers about machine setups and operational specifications, and provide training on machine operation as needed.
Select, clean, and install spacers, sleeves, and cutters on arbors; maintain machines in proper operational condition.
Test and adjust machine speeds or actions according to product specifications.
Inspect or measure completed workpieces to determine conformance to specifications, using measuring instruments such as gauges or micrometers.
Shape and fabricate metal structural parts such as plates, bulkheads, and frames, following design layouts.
Use knowledge of physical properties of materials to plan sequences of operations.

Inspect sample workpieces to verify conformance with specifications using instruments such as gauges, micrometers, and dial indicators; check to ensure that workpieces are properly lubricated and cooled during machine operation; verify alignment of workpieces on machines, using measuring instruments such as rules, gauges, or calipers; examine, inspect, and measure raw materials and finished products to verify conformance to specifications.

Replace worn tools and sharpen dull tools using bench grinders or cutter-grinding machines; replace worn machine parts, using hand tools, and notify engineering personnel when corrective action is required.

Maintain stocks of machine parts and machining tools; maintain machines and remove and replace broken or worn machine tools using hand tools; order tooling for jobs; maintain an inventory of materials.

Create functional or decorative objects by hand using a variety of methods and materials; sketch or draw objects to be crafted; develop concepts or creative ideas for craft objects; select materials based on strength, color, texture, weight, size, and other characteristics; apply finishes to crafted objects.

Study blueprints, layouts, sketches, or work orders to assess workpiece specifications; determine tooling instructions, tools and materials needed, and sequences of operations; select cutting tools and tooling instructions according to specifications and knowledge of metal properties; compute dimensions, tolerances, and angles of workpieces or machines according to specifications; adjust machine controls and change tool settings to keep dimensions within specified tolerances.

Set up, operate, or tend grinding and related tools that remove excess material or burrs from surfaces; start machines and set controls to regulate vacuum, air pressure, sizing rings, and temperature; manipulate controls to monitor, adjust, and regulate speeds of machine mechanisms.

Turn valves or pull levers to start and regulate the flow of coolant or lubricant to work areas; refill, change, and monitor the level of fluids in machines; control coolant systems; brush or spray lubricating compounds on workpieces, or direct coolant flow against tools and workpieces; lift and position workpieces manually or using hoists, secure them in machines using fasteners and hand tools; lift workpieces to machines manually or with hoists or cranes.

Set and adjust machine controls according to product specifications, using knowledge of machine operation; input initial part dimensions into machine control panels; program computer numerical control (CNC) machines and modify cutting programs to account for operational issues.

Observe machine operations to detect problems; making necessary adjustments to correct discrepancies; troubleshoot, maintain, and make minor repairs to equipment; monitor machine operation and control panel displays and compare readings to specifications to detect malfunctions.
Clean work area and machines, tooling, or parts using solvents or solutions and rags; remove and replace worn plastic block refractory linings of furnaces, using hand tools.

Confer with customers to assess needs or obtain feedback; develop product packaging, display, and pricing strategies; research craft trends, venues, and customer buying patterns to inspire designs and marketing strategies; advertise products and work using media such as internet advertising and brochures.

Activate shears and grinders to trim workpieces; signal and assist other workers to remove and position equipment, fill hoppers, and feed materials into machines; sort shop orders to maximize materials utilization and minimize machine setup time; stack or load finished items or place them on conveyor systems.

Move machine controls to index workpieces, and to adjust machines for pre-selected operational settings; adjust air cylinders and setting stops to set traverse lengths and feed arm strokes; slide spacers between buffs on spindles to set spacing; thread and hand-feed materials through machine cutters or abraders; disassemble sizing mills removed from rolling lines, and sort and store parts; use equipment designed to join sheet metal, such as spot welders; set specifications for materials, dimensions, and finishes; install equipment such as guides, guards, and rolls, using hand tools.
Develop designs using specialized computer software; prepare geometric layouts from graphic displays using computer-assisted drafting software or drafting instruments.

Lift metal stock or workpieces manually or using hoists, and position and secure them in machines, using fasteners and hand tools; adjust machine feed and speed, change cutting tools, or adjust machine controls when automatic programming is faulty or if machines malfunction; listen to machines during operation to detect sounds made by dull cutting tools or excessive vibration, and adjust machines to compensate for problems; clean and lubricate machines.

Remove workpieces from machines, and check to ensure they conform to specifications; record production output; examine, inspect, and measure raw materials and finished products to verify conformance to specifications; relocate workpieces as necessary.

Observe machines on trial runs or conduct computer simulations to ensure that programs and machinery will function properly and produce items that meet specifications. Revise programs or tapes to eliminate errors and retest programs to check that problems have been solved. Determine the sequence of machine operations, and select the proper cutting tools needed to machine workpieces into the desired shapes. Write simple programs for computer-controlled machine tools. Analyze job orders, drawings, blueprints, specifications, and design data to calculate dimensions, tool selection, machine speeds, and feed rates. Determine reference points, machine cutting paths, or hole locations, and compute angular and linear dimensions, radii, and curvatures.

Examine objects, systems, or facilities and analyze information to determine needed installations, services, or repairs. Inspect, test, and measure completed work, using devices such as hand tools or gauges to verify conformance to standards or repair requirements. Monitor tool and part inventories and the condition and maintenance of shops to ensure adequate working conditions. Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations, using equipment such as hand or power tools, hydraulic presses or shears, or welding equipment. Develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures.

Counsel employees about work-related issues and assist employees to correct job-skill deficiencies. Recommend or initiate personnel actions, such as hires, promotions, transfers, discharges, or disciplinary measures. Monitor employees' work levels and review work performance. Conduct or arrange for worker training in safety, repair, or maintenance techniques, operational procedures, or equipment use. Inspect and monitor work areas, examine tools and equipment, and provide employee safety training to prevent, detect, and correct unsafe conditions or violations of procedures and safety rules.

Requisition materials and supplies, such as tools, equipment, or replacement parts. Compute estimates and actual costs of factors such as materials, labor, or outside contractors. Develop or implement electronic maintenance programs or computer information management systems. Meet with vendors or suppliers to discuss products used in repair work. Participate in budget preparation and administration, coordinating purchasing and documentation and monitoring departmental expenditures. Compile operational or personnel records, such as time and production records, inventory data, repair or maintenance statistics, or test results.

Determine schedules, sequences, and assignments for work activities, based on work priority, quantity of equipment, and skill of personnel. Confer with personnel, such as management, engineering, quality control, customer, or union workers' representatives, to coordinate work activities, resolve employee grievances, or identify and review resource needs. Investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports of findings. Examine electronic components for defects or completeness of laser-beam trimming, using microscopes. Review, evaluate, accept, and coordinate completion of work bid from contractors. Design equipment configurations to meet personnel needs.

Lay out and mark areas of parts to be shot peened and fill hoppers with shot. This task is less relevant to the main responsibilities of metal working machine operatives and does not need to be retained due to its specificity and limited relevance.
